items.title: Journalistic Expression or Commercial Shaming? When Media Criticism Meets Commercial Disparagement Law
items.description: In a clash between legacy broadcast media and digital watchdog journalism, the Delhi High Court’s ruling in TV Today v. Newslaundry confronts a difficult modern question: when does sharp media criticism become actionable commercial disparagement? Naman Singh writes that while...

items.title: SpicyIP Bells & Whistles: IP Events and Opportunities (27.04.2026)
items.description: Welcome back to another week of Bells & Whistles. As always, we’ve rounded up a mix of developments, opportunities, and thoughtful reads from across the IP world along with a Bell of the Week that’s well worth revisiting. Bell of the Week: Rishab Aiyer Ghosh Some bells do not...

items.title: SpicyIP Weekly Review (April 20- April 26)
items.description: After an exciting week of discussion on GIs, ambush marketing, and the right of publicity, here is a round-up of the week with the latest edition of the Weekly Review for April. This week featured discussions on the Delhi High Court’s orders in the Peruvian Pisco appeal and the...

items.title: OSIM implementează Practicile Comune (PC16) și (PC17) începând cu 27 aprilie 2026
Bad-faith filing risks; sports trademark economy; Southeast Asia enforcement; and much more
items.description: Your Sunday recap of the week’s biggest developments in trademark and brand protection.

items.title: Crashing the Game, Not the Law: Ambush Marketing and IP Law
items.description: A very happy World IP Day to our readers! As the IPL frenzy returns, so does the battle for consumer attention, this time fought as much through witty notifications and real-time campaigns as on the cricket field, and raising familiar questions about the legality and limits of...

items.title: Teams, players, logos: how trademarks power the global sports economy
items.description: In honour of this year’s World IP Day, Clarivate’s Robert Reading examines the trademark portfolios of the world’s biggest sports teams, personalities and clothing brands.

items.title: The Cox Shadow Over Hikma: Three Questions for Wednesday’s Argument
items.description: Argument preview in Hikma v. Amarin: how Cox v. Sony reshapes the four key questions on skinny-label inducement under section 271(b). Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Never too late: If you missed the IPKat last week!
items.description: Kat napIn case last week was more naps than notes, the IPKat kept track. Here’s what you might have missed.CopyrightSöğüt Atilla-Aydın explored the Supreme Court’s take in Cox v Sony, stepping into the long-running tension between copyright enforcement and ISP responsibility,...

items.title: Saturday Sundries
items.description: While some readers may have started already counting down the days until summer, the IPKat is here to ensure you do not miss the latest news and forthcoming events in the meantime! Here are the highlights of the week: A Kat ready to read the weekly IP news,provided by her...

items.title: Why platforms must take greater responsibility to protect the future of sports broadcasting
items.description: This week’s Opinion Column examines the evolution and challenges of protecting sports broadcast rights.

items.title: Help us UK Supreme Court, you’re our only hope (for digital replicas)
items.description: Frames from Star Wars: A New Hope and Rogue One: A Star Wars Story - LucasfilmWho would have thought that Princess Leia’s ‘holographic’ plea in Star Wars: Episode IV – A New Hope would set the scene for an epic legal argument. Very quietly, over the last three years, a storm has...

items.title: World Intellectual Property Day 2026: New WIPO Data Show IP Use Booming in Sports Industry
items.description: Golf, swimming and racket sports are the most patent-intensive areas of the sports industry, where use of intellectual property (IP) is rising at above-average rates, according to a new World Intellectual Property Organization report released to mark World IP Day.

items.title: It’s the time to Pisco: Delhi HC Dismisses Peru’s GI Hopes Again!
items.description: “A Tale of Two Countries”. That is how the Delhi HC Division Bench (“DB”) described the judgment dated 18th March 2026 in the Appeal by the Embassy of Peru against the July 2025 Pisco decision by Justice Mini Pushkarna. Extensively covered on our blog previously, in travelling...

items.title: Pharma & Biotech Patent Litigation in Europe conference returns with IPKat readers’ discount
items.description: If, like this Kat, you love all things pharma, biotech and patents, then the Pharma & Biotech Patent Litigation in Europe conference is for you. The IPKat is also happy to report that there is a special 10% discount in the registration fee, with discount code: D10-999-IPKAT26....

items.title: Crystal Bar: Contempt application against litigant and its lawyers struck out
items.description: This Kat has covered the dispute between Shenzhen SKE Technology Ltd (SKE) and Bargain Busting Ltd (BBL) previously (IPKat here). It is a case that perhaps ought to be relatively simple, in that it is a battle over who has the earliest rights to the words CRYSTAL BAR in relation...

items.title: Nokia wins anti-suit injunction against Geely’s interim-licence application
items.description: The connected cars dispute between Nokia and Geely, which has been pending since last summer, came to a head this week. After Geely filed an application for an interim licence in China, Nokia successfully filed an anti-suit injunction against a Chinese interim licence with both...

items.title: Music labels drop $2.6 billion Verizon suit; Frida Kahlo dispute revived; Spotify win over shadow library – news digest
items.description: Six bite-sized IP updates from the week, also featuring YouTube’s deepfake detection tool for public figures, Seoul’s covert “mirror grade” counterfeit store bust, and Amazon’s AI-led protection efforts.

items.title: Huawei damages, AI videos, rapid-fire injunctions: five China IP cases you may have missed
items.description: Decisions on issues such as refurbished products, fake amusement park tickets and prior domain name rights reveal how Chinese courts are sharpening their approach to IP enforcement.

items.title: Fun While It Lasted: Judge Albright to Leave the Bench
items.description: Judge Alan Albright will resign from WDTX at the end of August, but the patent-venue shift to EDTX and Midland-Odessa already happened. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Hormuz disruption hits global stocks as WTR Brand Elite declines
items.description: The WTR Brand Elite index fell four points between 1 February and 1 April 2026, with a March sell-off triggered by the effective shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz wiping out earlier gains and sending shockwaves through global markets.

items.title: Counterfeiters, dupes and brand ownership risks: practical enforcement tips for Southeast Asia
items.description: Counterfeiters in Southeast Asia are producing convincing replicas, inventing “non‑existing models”, claiming OEM origin and using modest price reductions to deceive consumers and seize local trademarks, warns a senior IP specialist at audio electronics company HARMAN.

items.title: New technology makes bad-faith filings easier so brands must change tact, expert warns
items.description: Cases in which bad faith is central to the outcome are rising across major jurisdictions.

items.title: Win some, lose some: UPC upholds Dainese’s airbag patent but finds it not infringed
items.description: Almost exactly a year after the UPC’s Milan local division asserted its jurisdiction for Spain, it has ruled that the patent-in-suit EP 4 072 364 is valid but not infringed by Alpinestars’ products (case IDs: UPC_CFI_472/2024, UPC CFI 792/2024, UPC CFI 831/2024 and UPC CFI...

items.title: Recalibration: Seven Months of PTAB § 101 Data
items.description: Seven months of PTAB data confirm § 101 reversals have doubled under Squires, driven by incumbent judges changing behavior, not turnover. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Part II: Flower Nahi, Fire: Delhi HC Waters the Wrong Rights (Again!)
items.description: Building on Part I’s critique of the DHC’s doctrinal conflation in the Allu Arjun case, Part II of the post turns to a related concern: the Court’s failure to distinguish between vastly different forms of unauthorised use. In this post, Dr. Aakanksha Kumar explains how...

items.title: Part I: Flower Nahi, Fire: Delhi HC Waters the Wrong Rights (Again!)
items.description: Recent personality rights orders from the Delhi High Court continue to push the doctrine into uncertain territory, with the latest ruling in favour of Allu Arjun marking a particularly sharp turn. In Part I of the two-part post on the order, Dr. Aakanksha Kumar argues that by...

items.title: Paris local division revokes Michelin patent in Goodyear case
items.description: Michelin filed an infringement action at the Paris local division in February 2025. The patent was only valid in France at the time of the action. The company argued that its competitor Goodyear infringed its intellectual property rights with the car tyres Fuelmax D Endurance...

items.title: UK High Court sees an increase in new cases as tensions with UPC intensify
items.description: In recent years, the UK High Court — like other national courts — had seen a decrease in newly filed patent cases, reaching an all-time low in 2022. However, last year, according to the UK civil justice statistics, the UK High Court recorded significantly more cases than in...

items.title: [Guest post] The well-known status of a non-European trade mark in the EU: The Frisby case
items.description: The IPKat has received and is pleased to host the following guest contribution by Katfriend Salvatore Marcenaro (Wolf Méndez Abogados Asociados) providing updates on the Frisby case. Here’s what Salvatore writes: The well-known status of a non-European trade mark in the EU: The...

items.title: Risks associated with UDRP cancellation remedy after L’Oréal cancels 705 domain names
items.description: Of the two remedies that exist under the UDRP, cancellation is much less popular since the domains can re-enter circulation by bad actors and financial risks are high. L’Oréal’s decision to pursue this action is thus unusual and warrants a closer look at different brands’...

items.title: US Congress urged to adopt Pro Codes Act as courts unsettle copyright status of incorporated standards
items.description: The incentive for creating high-quality industry standards is being threatened by recent ‘fair use’ decisions, experts tell the House IP subcommittee.

items.title: HGF tech practice receives another boost with Barker Brettell partner
items.description: UK and European patent attorney Oliver Pooley has joined HGF’s Birmingham office. He will bolster the firm’s tech and engineering team. Pooley has a degree in physics and chemistry and a PhD in electronic and electrical engineering. His technical specialties include electronics,...

items.title: Sekisui and Bird & Bird deal another blow to competitor Kuraray
items.description: Two Japanese companies are fighting it out in German courts. This is rare, but the dispute between Sekisui Chemicals and Kuraray is particularly intense. Sekisui Chemicals has filed four lawsuits based on four patents against its Japanese competitor Kuraray in Germany. The two...

items.title: Still Climbing: PTA Hits 318 Days, Back to 2015 Levels
items.description: Patent Term Adjustment hits 318-day peak in April 2026, back to 2015 levels. Director Squires' oldest-cases-first push is part of the story. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: CMS strengthens London IP practice with McCarthy Denning partner
items.description: Tom Carver officially joined CMS Cameron McKenna’s London practice on 20 April as partner. He brings experience in patents, trademarks, and design rights. In addition to law, Carver also holds a degree in genetics from the University of Nottingham. He has been involved in...

items.title: Brita prevails again in filter cartridge dispute
items.description: Water filters are popular in households to remove certain substances, such as limescale, from tap water prior to consumption. The filters in water carafes are typically replaced at regular intervals to achieve the desired purity. Manufacturer Brita is currently taking action...

items.title: The DTSA’s Missing Preemption: Does § 1835(b) Bind State Courts?
items.description: A new cert petition asks whether the DTSA's trade secret confidentiality protections in 18 U.S.C. § 1835(b) bind state courts. The answer is far from clear. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: SpicyIP Bells & Whistles: IP Events and Opportunities (21.04.2026)
items.description: Welcome back to another week of Bells & Whistles. Before we get into this week’s Bell — we’ve just started a SpicyIP WhatsApp group to share updates and opportunities. Would be great to have you there (click the link to join the channel)! And as always, we’ve rounded up a mix of...

items.title: Daren Tang Appointed for Second Term as WIPO Director General
items.description: The Member States of the World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) today appointed Daren Tang to a second six-year term of office as Director General of the Organization.

items.title: The Inventor is still Human: Indian Patent Office’s DABUS Refusal
items.description: In keeping with a broad global trend, the Indian Patent Office has refused Dr. Stephen Thaler’s patent application which sought to recognise Dr. Thaler’s AI system DABUS as the inventor of a ‘food container and devices and methods for attracting enhanced attention’. The Indian...

items.title: Barry v. DePuy Returns: En Banc Petition Tests EcoFactor’s Reach on Rule 702 Gatekeeping
items.description: DePuy's en banc petition in Barry v. DePuy Synthes argues the panel reintroduced the weight-not-admissibility framework that EcoFactor and the 2023 Rule 702 amendments rejected. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Merz and A&O gain sales ban for generic fampridine products in Germany
items.description: Merz is fighting in Germany and at the UPC over its drug Fampyra, which aims to improve walking ability in multiple sclerosis patients. At the centre of the dispute are EP 2 377 536 and EP 1 732 548. Both cover methods of using sustained release aminopyridine compositions and...

items.title: EU General Court rejects opposition to Irish Grass Fed Beef PGI
items.description: Last week, the EU General Court dismissed an opposition to the Irish Grass Fed Beef PGI in Case T-62/24. A group called the Independent Farmers Of Ireland (IFOI) opposed the Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) on the grounds that they were deprived of procedural rights to...

items.title: AIR-1, Right of Publicity-0: Delhi HC’s CLATastrophic Mix-Up
items.description: This post is co-authored with Dr. Aakanksha Kumar. Dr. Aakanksha Kumar (She/Her) is an independent researcher and academic who also consults with content creators and advises Chhattisgarhi music artists on copyright-related matters. Previously, she served as Associate Professor,...

items.title: Another win for Abbott as Court of Appeal grants PI against Sinocare
items.description: Abbott thus wins outright at the UPC in the second instance. The company filed two PI applications with The Hague local division in June 2024. Abbott alleges Sinocare and its distribution partner Menarini infringe its EP 4 344 633 and EP 3 988 471 and seeks to halt sales of...

items.title: Neuschwansteiner – When a fairy-tale castle casts its spell on trade mark law
items.description: Can the mystique of a world-famous castle prevent its name from functioning as a trade mark? In its Neuschwansteiner decision (case 26 W (pat) 34/17), the German Patent Court ("GPC") grappled with the case law of the Court of Justice of the EU ("CJEU") on geographical...

items.title: Same Problem, Same Solution: Reading Trade Secrets Across Fields
items.description: Federal Circuit reverses $17M Penuma judgment, tightening California's 'generally known' trade secret rule and tying it to patent inventorship. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Champagne problems? Punk solutions Amyl and the Sniffers-style
items.description: Champagne Problem Series Image‘Hey! When you get down, oh you’re a lit one,Never been a dull one, Always been a big star’(Amyl and the Sniffers, Big Dreams)Big star indeed, Amy Louise Taylor. Musician, activist and all-round punk extraordinaire, best known as the songwriter and...

items.title: Extra Credit Not Required: Teva v. Lilly and the Limits of Amgen’s Reach
items.description: Federal Circuit reverses JMOL in Teva v. Lilly, holding that method-of-use claims face a lower Section 112 bar when the genus is well known and not itself the invention. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Never Too Late: If you missed the IPKat last week!
items.description: It was a lively week on the IPKat, with a set of posts full of insights worth your attention. In case you didn’t catch everything, here’s your round-up: Fordham IP Conference Kat taking a short nap between conference sessionsKatfriends Sarah Bui and Seth B. Pearson took us to...

items.title: Extraordinary by Design: How the USPTO Is Bypassing Its Own Reexamination Rules
items.description: USPTO's new pre-order procedure lets patent owners oppose ex parte reexamination before the SNQ determination, but the legal basis is shaky. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: Mind the Gap: The Middle Layer of Obviousness Doctrine
items.description: Graham's four factors organize the evidence but don't resolve obviousness. The doctrines built atop them do the real analytical work. Continue reading this post on Patently-O.

items.title: WIPO Launches New Award for Cities of Innovation
items.description: The World Intellectual Property Organization (WIPO) today launched the “WIPO City of Innovation” award program, a new initiative that recognizes cities worldwide demonstrating exceptional commitment to fostering innovation and creativity among their residents.

items.title: WIPO Launches Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange
items.description: WIPO today launched the Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Interchange (AIII), a new initiative for expert dialogue on IP and AI issues that will focus on technical and operational issues and complement ongoing discussions at other WIPO forums.

items.title: Albania is First to Join WIPO’s Riyadh Design Law Treaty
items.description: Albania today became the first member state to join WIPO’s Riyadh Design Law Treaty (RDLT), depositing its instrument of ratification with Director General Daren Tang and marking a major milestone in bringing into force the new international framework for design protection.

items.title: International Patent Applications Rose in 2025, with Digital Communication and Semiconductor Technologies Showing Strong Growth
items.description: International patent applications filed through WIPO's Patent Cooperation Treaty (PCT) rose 0.7% in 2025 to reach 275,900 filings worldwide, marking a second consecutive year of growth. The increase was partly due to robust activity in ICT related fields, with digital...
